PR surface: Source +149, Tests +74. Total +223 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is feature-style diagnostic exposure rather than a bug report. The PR body provides after-fix source CLI output for explicit `--only` selection and unchanged default lint selection.

target.title: Doctor: expose device pairing findings
target.body:
## Summary

- expose existing device-pairing Doctor diagnostics as structured `core/doctor/device-pairing` findings
- keep the structured lint check default-disabled, so broad `doctor --lint` behavior is unchanged unless operators use `--all` or explicit `--only`
- keep real device-pairing behavior in the legacy Doctor note path; the structured detector is read-only and does not probe the Gateway in lint mode

## Public contract

No public SDK, plugin manifest, config schema, CLI flag, or persisted data-model contract changes.

## Validation

- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-device-pairing.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (63 tests across 2 shards)
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-device-pairing.ts src/commands/doctor-device-pairing.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-device-pairing.ts src/commands/doctor-device-pairing.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm tsgo:core`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm plugin-sdk:check-exports`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Expose the existing device-pairing Doctor diagnostics through explicit structured lint selection without changing broad `doctor --lint` defaults or probing Gateway from lint.

Real environment tested:
Ubuntu 24.04 WSL root, source checkout at `eff0d3cdae1ddab01f5daf7546c245f78a2ebd91`, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, no live Gateway service required for the quiet-path lint proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<temp>/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm openclaw doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/device-pairing`
2. `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<temp>/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm openclaw doctor --lint --json`

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "onlyExit": 0,
  "onlyChecksRun": 1,
  "onlyChecksSkipped": 35,
  "onlyDeviceFindings": 0,
  "defaultExit": 1,
  "defaultDeviceFindings": 0
}
```

Observed result after fix:
Explicit `--only core/doctor/device-pairing` runs the new structured check and exits cleanly against isolated quiet state. Broad default lint still emits no `core/doctor/device-pairing` findings, preserving the default-disabled contract.

What was not tested:
No live Gateway pairing service was exercised in this refresh; existing focused tests cover pending scope upgrades, corrupt local pairing state, stale cached device tokens, sanitization, and structured conversion.
